title: Trump Says Negotiations With Iran Will Continue as U.S. Ramps Up Strikes
summary: U.S. forces hit more than 170 targets, including air defense systems, drone and missile storage sites, and military speedboats.

title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Remains Absent, a Void at the Top of the Regime
summary: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei did not appear at his father’s funeral this week, fueling speculation about his physical condition and leaving a power vacuum in a divided country.

title: Shippers Face Deepening Dilemma as U.S. and Iran Vie for Control of the Strait
summary: Companies desperately want to get their ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz, but face mounting risks. “Things are becoming uglier by the minute,” one executive said.

title: Mediators Trying to Pull U.S. and Iran Back From Brink, Officials Say
summary: Qatari mediators were in Iran seeking to salvage the tattered truce. President Trump said talks with Tehran would continue, even if the cease-fire was “over.”

title: Trump Cuts Habitat Protections for Endangered Species
summary: The move could open up habitats to drilling and mining, and hasten the demise of many imperiled animals, experts say.

title: Trump Administration Taps Climate Science Critic to Oversee Flagship Report
summary: Matthew M. Wielicki has criticized the scientific consensus on global warming as alarmist. Now he’ll oversee a major federal report on the topic.

title: He Wanted to Track Microplastics in the Sea. The E.P.A. Fired Him.
summary: The official urged requiring a fish farm to monitor for the tiny plastic particles. The E.P.A. said he had taken steps to embarrass the agency.

title: Witnesses of ICE Killing in Houston Dispute the Official Account
summary: The agency said a Mexican immigrant driving a van tried to ram agents before they shot him dead. A lawyer for three passengers said that was untrue.

title: At Least 21 People Have Been Shot at by Federal Immigration Agents Since Last Year
summary: Many of them have been shot in their cars. Five have died, including three U.S. citizens. Here are their names and what we know about the circumstances.

title: ICE Deports Immigrant Who Was Pardoned for Sex Abuse Conviction
summary: Minnesota state officials had granted a pardon to Tou Lue Vang, a Hmong man, drawing criticism that Democratic leaders were thwarting efforts to expel criminals.

title: Trump Says He Won’t Sign Housing Bill, in Protest Over Stalled Voting Measure
summary: The president did not say he would veto the housing legislation, so it would still become law unless he does. But his pronouncement reflects a growing rift between him and Senate Republicans.

title: New Air Force One Lacks Defensive Countermeasures of Previous Model, Officials Say
summary: Experts said the lack of such capabilities poses a potential risk when the president travels overseas. The White House defended the aircraft’s safety.

title: Democrats Haunted by Biden-Harris Switch as They Replace Platner in Maine
summary: The last-minute scramble to name a replacement reminds some in the party of the challenges they faced in 2024.

title: Why Some Women in Maine Are Mourning the End of Graham Platner’s Campaign
summary: They believe the accuser, but they also grieve the demise of a campaign that promised that politics could be different — and they blame those who failed to find a less flawed candidate.

title: Eight Years Later, They’re Still Restoring a House Bought at Auction
summary: First-time buyers from the Bay Area won an abandoned house at a Kingston, N.Y., tax auction. Eight years later, they’re still restoring it.

title: An Aging Assassin Was Caught by ICE. Will the U.S. Deport Him?
summary: He helped plot a car bombing in Washington that killed two people in 1976. Fifty years later, old government secrets could decide his fate.

title: Man Arrested on Suspicion of Murdering Ann Widdecombe, Former U.K. Politician
summary: The police said they had arrested a 26-year-old British man after Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former lawmaker, was found dead with serious injuries on Thursday.

title: The Work of Helping A.I. Destroy Work
summary: Start-ups are paying white-collar professionals to teach their jobs to artificial intelligence models. It’s a bonanza. It’s bleak. Where will it end?

title: Trump Is Promoting ‘Freedom Fuel’ for $3.47 a Gallon. Who’s Behind It?
summary: The president has praised a network of 25 recently rebranded gas stations in the Philadelphia region that have been selling gas for considerably less than the national average.

title: This Star Just Ate a Planet, and It’s Not Done Yet
summary: A star 1,300 light-years away appears to have just consumed one world and is gearing up for seconds.

title: A Chinese Spacecraft Captures First Image of Quasi-Moon
summary: A Chinese spacecraft has captured the first image of the asteroid Kamo‘oalewa.

title: This Is a Lot More Worrying Than the Supreme Court’s Ruling on Executive Power
summary: The White House is sidestepping Congress to make huge decisions for even the most important private industries.

title: The Beautiful Truth About Helen of Troy
summary: She was a legendary beauty, but also so much more.

title: How Volkswagen’s Troubles Were Made in China
summary: The German auto giant’s push into China powered it for decades, but now the company faces fierce competition from Chinese automakers in markets around the world.
